data.headline: Kitchen Remodeling in Greenwich, CT
data.subheadline: Licensed Connecticut kitchen remodeling contractor serving Greenwich and Fairfield County , full-service renovations from $30,000 to $150,000+.

data.title: Full-Service Kitchen Remodeling in Greenwich, CT
data.content:
Kitchen remodeling in Greenwich typically ranges from $30,000 for a basic cabinet and countertop update to $150,000 or more for a full custom renovation with structural changes. Most projects take six to twelve weeks of active construction, plus cabinet lead time. We hold CT HIC License #0668405 and serve Greenwich through our Fairfield County Service Area Team.

Greenwich is one of the wealthiest communities in the United States. The housing stock reflects this , large estates, stately colonials, Tudors, and custom builds spanning every decade from the 1920s to present. Many of these homes have high-end kitchens that are now 20-40 years old, with upgraded materials that were top of the line when installed but now need updating.

Greenwich homeowners expect clear proposals, daily updates during construction, and a project that finishes on the schedule we quoted. That's exactly how we operate.

data.title: What Is Included in a Kitchen Remodel in Greenwich
data.content:
A full kitchen remodel in Greenwich covers every trade from demolition through final paint: cabinetry, countertops, tile, flooring, electrical, plumbing, appliance installation, and all required Greenwich Building Department permits and inspections. Kitchens in Greenwich range from large rooms in pre-war estates to mid-century layouts in well-maintained Colonials, and the common thread is that expectations for fit and finish are high and custom work is the norm rather than the exception.

We start with demolition and removal of existing cabinets, countertops, and flooring. Before any new material goes in, we assess what is behind the walls. Greenwich homes built before the 1950s are often plaster-walled, and that plaster construction requires careful scribing of new cabinetry to achieve tight fits against irregular surfaces. Older estates can also reveal outdated electrical panels and original cast-iron plumbing that we bring up to current Connecticut code before the renovation moves forward.

New cabinetry installation includes soft-close hardware and adjustable shelving as standard. In Greenwich kitchens where custom or semi-custom cabinetry is specified, we scribe stiles and filler pieces tightly against plaster walls so the finished installation looks purpose-built for the room. Countertop fabrication and installation, backsplash tile, and underlayment preparation are all part of the project. Electrical scope covers new circuits for appliances, under-cabinet lighting, and updated outlet and switch locations.

Plumbing includes sink and dishwasher connections, garbage disposal installation, and any relocated water lines. Greenwich kitchens frequently include pot-filler rough-ins and island sink plumbing, and we handle both the supply and drain work for those additions. We coordinate appliance delivery and installation so you are not managing that separately.

Interior painting, drywall patching, and daily cleanup are included throughout. We install dust barriers to protect the rest of your home during construction. The project closes with a final walkthrough where we go through every detail together.

data.title: What Makes Kitchen Remodeling in Greenwich Different
data.content: Greenwich kitchens tend to be larger and more complex than typical Connecticut projects. Island configurations, professional-grade appliance suites, custom cabinetry with specialty finishes, and upgraded stone countertops are standard expectations here. Many homes in Belle Haven, Riverside, and Old Greenwich have kitchens that were designed for entertaining and require renovation approaches that preserve that function while modernizing systems and finishes.

data.title: Greenwich Building Department and Permits
data.content: The Greenwich Building Department is located at 101 Field Point Road. Greenwich has specific zoning requirements including setback rules, floor area ratio limits, and coastal regulations for waterfront properties. Kitchen renovations that involve structural modifications, electrical upgrades, or plumbing changes require permits. The review process in Greenwich can take 2-4 weeks depending on scope. We handle all permit applications and coordinate every required inspection.

rows.notes: New cabinets, stone countertops, new flooring, appliances, updated lighting

rows.label: High-End

data.title: Kitchen Remodeling Cost in Greenwich, CT
data.subtitle: Kitchen remodeling in Greenwich ranges from $30,000 for a basic cabinet and countertop refresh to $150,000 or more for a full custom renovation with structural changes. Ranges are illustrative for typical Connecticut projects. Final pricing depends on scope, materials, site conditions, and permit fees. Detailed written estimate provided before any work begins.

data.title: Why Greenwich Kitchen Remodeling Requires Local Expertise
data.content:
Greenwich homes present construction conditions that contractors without local experience regularly miss.

Greenwich kitchens built in the 1980s and 1990s often have complex layouts with butler's pantries, breakfast rooms, and dedicated appliance garages that need modernizing. Older homes in backcountry Greenwich may have well water systems that affect plumbing scope. Waterfront properties in Riverside and Old Greenwich present moisture considerations. We assess these conditions during the initial consultation and account for them in every proposal.

The upgraded expectations in Greenwich mean that material selections, craftsmanship standards, and project management need to meet a higher bar. We deliver that consistently.

data.title: Our Kitchen Remodeling Process
data.subtitle: Every Greenwich kitchen remodel follows the same five-step process. This structure keeps projects on schedule, on budget, and clearly communicated throughout.

steps.title: Consultation
steps.short: Visit your Greenwich home or connect by video
steps.description: We visit your Greenwich home or connect via Google Meet or Zoom to discuss your goals, assess the space, and answer your questions. No charge. No obligation. We note the existing electrical and plumbing conditions upfront.

steps.title: Planning
steps.short: Clear written proposal and line items
steps.description: You receive a clear written proposal covering exactly what's included, how long it will take, and what it costs. We break out cabinetry, countertops, tile, electrical, plumbing, flooring, and Greenwich Building Department permits separately so you understand exactly where the budget is going.

steps.title: Selections
steps.short: Materials and lead times locked in early
steps.description: We guide you through material choices with options at different price points. We communicate lead times clearly so selections are made on schedule before construction begins. If a material has a long lead time, we flag that immediately.

steps.title: Build
steps.description: Construction begins on the agreed schedule. You receive daily updates on progress, a clean job site at the end of every workday, and crews who arrive when scheduled. If something unexpected comes up behind a wall, we contact you that day and present your options.

steps.title: Walkthrough
steps.description: We walk through the finished project together. We check every cabinet, every drawer, every tile, every light fixture. If anything needs attention, we address it before calling the project complete. Your written acceptance at the final walkthrough is the last step.

data.title: Project Timeline
data.subtitle: Most kitchen remodels take ten to twenty-two weeks from signed proposal to final walkthrough.

items.title: Planning & Design
items.description: 1-2 Weeks. Consultation, measurements, material selections, and detailed proposal.

items.title: Cabinet Lead Time
items.description: 4-12 Weeks. Custom orders require the longest lead time. Semi-custom options arrive in four to six weeks.

items.title: Construction
items.description: 6-12 Weeks. Demo, rough-ins, cabinetry, countertops, tile, flooring, and fixtures.

items.title: Final Touches
items.description: 1 Week. Hardware, final connections, punch list, and walkthrough.

items.question: Do I need a permit for kitchen remodeling in Greenwich?
items.answer: Yes. Most kitchen remodeling projects in Greenwich require permits from the Greenwich Building Department, particularly when the work involves electrical, plumbing, or structural modifications. We handle all permit applications and coordinate every required inspection as part of the project; you do not need to manage that process yourself.

items.question: How much does kitchen remodeling cost in Greenwich?
items.answer: Kitchen remodeling in Greenwich typically ranges from $30,000 for a basic cabinet and countertop refresh to $150,000 or more for a full custom renovation with structural changes. A mid-range project with new cabinets, stone countertops, new flooring, and updated lighting generally falls between $60,000 and $100,000. Your written proposal includes a detailed cost breakdown by trade so you know exactly where the budget is going.

items.question: How long does a kitchen remodel take in Greenwich?
items.answer: Active construction on a Greenwich kitchen remodel takes six to twelve weeks depending on the scope of work. Cabinet lead time needs to be added to that figure: custom cabinets require eight to twelve weeks, semi-custom four to six weeks, and stock cabinets are available within one to two weeks. Your written proposal includes a complete project schedule that accounts for all lead times.

items.question: What types of homes do you remodel kitchens in for Greenwich?
items.answer: Greenwich's housing stock is primarily estates, colonials, Tudor-style, and custom-built homes built between the 1920s through 1990s. We're familiar with the construction conditions common in these homes and account for them in every proposal and project scope.

items.question: What is included in a kitchen remodel in Greenwich?
items.answer: A full-scope kitchen remodel in Greenwich includes demolition and removal of all existing cabinetry, countertops, and flooring; structural assessment; new cabinetry installation with hardware; countertop fabrication and installation; backsplash tile work; new flooring; all electrical work including new circuits and lighting; all plumbing including sink, dishwasher, and disposal connections; appliance coordination and installation; interior painting; all drywall patching and finish work; Greenwich Building Department permit applications and inspection coordination; daily cleanup; and a final walkthrough.
